title: Wound dressing layer for improved fluid removal
abstract: Disclosed herein are embodiments of a wound dressing layer. The wound dressing layer may include an open-cell foam. The foam may have a 50% compression force deflection of not more than about 4.8 kPa. The foam may also have a density of at least 24 kg/m 3 . The foam may exhibit a density increase of a factor of at least 12 at about -75 mmHg.
claims: Claims CLAIMS What is claimed is: 1. A wound dressing layer comprising an open-cell foam having a 50% compression force deflection of not more than about 4.8 kPa, a density of at least 24 kg/m 3 , and exhibiting a density increase of a factor of at least 12 at about -75 mmHg. 2. The wound dressing layer of claim 1, having a 50% compression force deflection of not more than about 4.5 kPa. 3. The wound dressing layer of any of claims 1-2, having a 50% compression force deflection of not more than about 4.0 kPa. 4. The wound dressing layer of any of claims 1-3, having a 50% compression force deflection of not more than about 3.5 kPa. 5. The wound dressing layer of any of claims 1-4, having a 50% compression force deflection of not more than about 3.0 kPa. 6. The wound dressing layer of any of claims 1-5, having a density of at least 28 kg/m 3 . 7. The wound dressing layer of any of claims 1-6, having a density of at least 32 kg/m 3 . 8. The wound dressing layer of any of claims 1-7, having a density of at least 36 kg/m 3 . 9. The wound dressing layer of any of claims 1-8, having a density of at least 40 kg/m 3 . 10. The wound dressing layer of any of claim 1-9, exhibiting a density increase of a factor of at least 5 at about -25 mmHg. 11. The wound dressing layer of any of claims 1-10, exhibiting a density increase of a factor of at least 9 at about -50 mmHg. 12. The wound dressing layer of any of claims 1-11, exhibiting a density increase of a factor of at least 14 at about -100 mmHg. 13. The wound dressing layer of any of claims 1-12, exhibiting a density increase of a factor of at least 16 at about -125 mmHg. 14. The wound dressing layer of any of claims 1-13, wherein the open-cell foam is hydrophobic. 15. The wound dressing layer of any of claims 1-14, wherein the open-cell foam is formed from polyurethane. 16. A negative-pressure system for providing negative-pressure therapy to a tissue site, the system comprising: a wound dressing layer comprising an open-cell foam having a 50% compression force deflection of not more than about 4.8 kPa, a density of at least 24 kg/m 3 , and exhibiting a density increase of a factor of at least 12 at about -75 mmHg; and a cover configured to be placed over the wound dressing layer and attached to tissue surrounding the tissue site; and a negative-pressure source configured to be fluidly coupled to the wound dressing layer. 17. The negative-pressure system of claim 16, having a 50% compression force deflection of not more than about 4.5 kPa. 18. The negative-pressure system of any of claims 16-17, having a 50% compression force deflection of not more than about 4.0 kPa. 19. The negative-pressure system of any of claims 16-18, having a 50% compression force deflection of not more than about 3.5 kPa. 20. The negative-pressure system of any of claims 16-19, having a 50% compression force deflection of not more than about 3.0 kPa. 21. The negative-pressure system of any of claims 16-20, having a density of at least 28 kg/m 3 . 22. The negative-pressure system of any of claims 16-21, having a density of at least 32 kg/m 3 . 23. The negative-pressure system of any of claims 16-22, having a density of at least 36 kg/m 3 . 24. The negative-pressure system of any of claims 16-23, having a density of at least 40 kg/m 3 . 25. The negative-pressure system of any of claim 16-24, exhibiting a density increase of a factor of at least 5 at about -25 mmHg. 26. The negative-pressure system of any of claims 16-25, exhibiting a density increase of a factor of at least 9 at about -50 mmHg. 27. The negative-pressure system of any of claims 16-26, exhibiting a density increase of a factor of at least 14 at about -100 mmHg. 28. The negative-pressure system of any of claims 16-27, exhibiting a density increase of a factor of at least 16 at about -125 mmHg. 29. The negative-pressure system of any of claims 16-28, wherein the open-cell foam is hydrophobic. 30. The negative-pressure system of any of claims 16-29, wherein the open-cell foam is formed from polyurethane. 31. The negative-pressure system of any of claims 16-30, wherein the negative-pressure source is an open loop negative-pressure source. 32. The negative-pressure system of any of claims 16-31, wherein the negative-pressure source is not configured to be controlled in response to a signal from a pressure sensor associated with the sealed space. 33. A method for providing negative-pressure therapy to a tissue site, the method comprising: positioning a wound dressing layer comprising an open-cell foam having a 50% compression force deflection of not more than about 4.8 kPa, a density of at least 24 kg/m 3 , and exhibiting a density increase of a factor of at least 12 at about -75 mmHg proximate to the tissue site; placing a sealing member over the wound dressing layer and sealing the sealing member to tissue surrounding the tissue site to form a sealed space; fluidly coupling a negative-pressure source to the sealed space; and operating the negative-pressure source to draw fluid from the tissue site through the wound dressing layer and generate a negative-pressure in the sealed space. 34. The method of claim 33, having a 50% compression force deflection of not more than about 4.5 kPa. 35. The method of any of claims 33-34, having a 50% compression force deflection of not more than about 4.0 kPa. 36. The method of any of claims 33-35, having a 50% compression force deflection of not more than about 3.5 kPa. 37. The method of any of claims 33-36, having a 50% compression force deflection of not more than about 3.0 kPa. 38. The method of any of claims 33-37, having a density of at least 28 kg/m 3 . 39. The method of any of claims 33-38, having a density of at least 32 kg/m 3 . 40. The method of any of claims 33-39, having a density of at least 36 kg/m 3 . 41. The method of any of claims 33-40, having a density of at least 40 kg/m 3 . 42. The method of any of claim 33-41, exhibiting a density increase of a factor of at least 5 at about -25 mmHg. 43. The method of any of claims 33-42, exhibiting a density increase of a factor of at least 9 at about -50 mmHg. 44. The method of any of claims 33-43, exhibiting a density increase of a factor of at least 14 at about -100 mmHg. 45. The method of any of claims 33-44, exhibiting a density increase of a factor of at least 16 at about -125 mmHg. 46. The method of any of claims 33-5, wherein the open-cell foam is hydrophobic. 47. The method of any of claims 33-46, wherein the open-cell foam is formed from polyurethane. 48. The method of any of claims 33-47, wherein the negative-pressure system does not receive feedback from a pressure sensor associated with the sealed space. 49. The method of any of claims 33-48, wherein the negative-pressure source is an open loop negative-pressure source.
